perturbation.original_text: 2. The Information is of a proprietary and confidential nature, the disclosure of which to any other party could result in damages to the Seller and/or Business. (a) The Information furnished by Broker or Seller has not been publicly disclosed, has not been made available to Buyer by any party or source other than Broker or Seller and is being furnished only upon the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement; (b) Buyer will not disclose the Information, in whole or in part, to any party other than persons within Buyer's organization, including independent advisers/consultants, who have a need to know such Information for purposes of evaluating or structuring the possible purchase of the Business. Buyer accepts full responsibility for full compliance with all provisions of this Agreement by such other persons; (c) Buyer will not disclose, except to the extent required by law, to any parties other than the persons described in Paragraph 2(B) above that the Business is available for purchase or that evaluations, discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible purchase; (d) Buyer will not utilize, now or at any time in the future, any trade secret(s), as that term may be defined under statutory or common law, that is/are included in the furnished Information for any purpose other than evaluating the possible purchase of the Business, including, without limitation, not utilizing same in the conduct of Buyer's or any other party's present or future business(es); (e) In addition to the prohibition against utilizing trade secret(s), Buyer will not utilize any other furnished information for any purpose other than evaluating the possible purchase of the Business; and (f) If Buyer decides does not purchase the Business, Buyer will promptly return to Broker all Information previously furnished by Broker or Seller, including any and all reproductions of same, and further, shall destroy any and all analyses, compilations or other material that incorporates any part of said Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The Information is of a proprietary and confidential nature, the disclosure of which to any other party could result in damages to the Seller and/or Business. (a) The Information furnished by Broker or Seller has not been publicly disclosed, has not been made available to Buyer by any party or source other than Broker or Seller and is being furnished only upon the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement; (b) Buyer may disclose the Information, in whole or in part, to any party within Buyer's organization, including independent advisers/consultants, without restriction. Buyer is not responsible for compliance with any provisions of this Agreement by such other persons; (c) Buyer may disclose, without restriction, to any parties that the Business is available for purchase or that evaluations, discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible purchase; (d) Buyer may utilize, now or at any time in the future, any trade secret(s), as that term may be defined under statutory or common law, that is/are included in the furnished Information for any purpose, including utilizing same in the conduct of Buyer's or any other party's present or future business(es); (e) Buyer may utilize any other furnished information for any purpose; and (f) If Buyer decides does not purchase the Business, Buyer is not obligated to return to Broker all Information previously furnished by Broker or Seller, including any and all reproductions of same, and further, is not obligated to destroy any and all analyses, compilations or other material that incorporates any part of said Information.
perturbation.explanation: This complete rewrite of section 2 removes almost all confidentiality restrictions, directly contradicting the initial statement that the information is 'of a proprietary and confidential nature' and that disclosure could result in damages. The original text set clear expectations regarding non-disclosure, limited use, and return of information; the revised text completely undermines these protections, creating an in-text contradiction as the opening sentence states that information is confidential, which is then immediately negated by the rest of the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Information is of a proprietary and confidential nature, the disclosure of which to any other party could result in damages to the Seller and/or Business. (a) The Information furnished by Broker or Seller has not been publicly disclosed, has not been made available to Buyer by any party or source other than Broker or Seller and is being furnished only upon the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement; (b) Buyer may disclose the Information, in whole or in part, to any party within Buyer's organization, including independent advisers/consultants, without restriction. Buyer is not responsible for compliance with any provisions of this Agreement by such other persons; (c) Buyer may disclose, without restriction, to any parties that the Business is available for purchase or that evaluations, discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible purchase; (d) Buyer may utilize, now or at any time in the future, any trade secret(s), as that term may be defined under statutory or common law, that is/are included in the furnished Information for any purpose, including utilizing same in the conduct of Buyer's or any other party's present or future business(es); (e) Buyer may utilize any other furnished information for any purpose; and (f) If Buyer decides does not purchase the Business, Buyer is not obligated to return to Broker all Information previously furnished by Broker or Seller, including any and all reproductions of same, and further, is not obligated to destroy any and all analyses, compilations or other material that incorporates any part of said Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by removing confidentiality restrictions and obligations. The original text states the information is proprietary and confidential, while the changed text allows for unrestricted disclosure and use of the information. The contradiction is evident in the complete rewrite of section 2, making it incompatible with the original intent and creating an in-text contradiction as the opening sentence states that information is confidential, which is then immediately negated by the rest of the section.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Buyer will not contact the Seller or Seller's employees, customers, suppliers or agents other than Broker for any reason whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Broker. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties will be made through or by Broker unless otherwise agreed to by Broker, in writing. Seller’s employees may not be aware of the sale of the Business and Buyer agrees not to disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, nor identify that Buyer is a possible purchaser of the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Buyer is encouraged to contact the Seller or Seller's employees, customers, suppliers or agents directly and without the need for the Broker's prior written consent. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties can be made directly by the Buyer. Buyer is expected to disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, and identify that Buyer is a possible purchaser of the Business.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause preventing the buyer from contacting the seller's stakeholders without the broker's consent. The revised text actively encourages direct contact and disclosure, potentially undermining the broker's role and the seller's confidentiality. The original and revised texts have opposing statements that contradicts in the meaning that is being given. This creates a clear in-text contradiction within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Buyer is encouraged to contact the Seller or Seller's employees, customers, suppliers or agents directly and without the need for the Broker's prior written consent. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties can be made directly by the Buyer. Buyer is expected to disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, and identify that Buyer is a possible purchaser of the Business.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3 directly contradicts the original intent by encouraging the buyer to contact the seller's stakeholders without the broker's consent, and to disclose the sale. The contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text because the changed text is copied onto the contradicted text) now opposes the original clause. This creates a clear and strong in-text contradiction based on the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition, as the revised text actively encourages direct contact and disclosure, while the original text prohibits this, undermining the broker's role and the seller's confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: 4. The Information furnished by Broker has been prepared by or is based upon representations of the Seller and Broker has made no independent investigation or verification of said Information. Broker makes no representation that the Information is accurate or complete. Buyer hereby expressly releases and discharges Broker from any and all responsibility and/or liability in connection with the accuracy, completeness or any other aspects of the information and accepts sole and final responsibility for the evaluation of the Information and all other factors relating to the Business. Buyer understands and acknowledges that it has been advised to engage attorneys, accountants and other advisors to complete due diligence on the Business offered for sale to verify that the Business is satisfactory. Buyer understands that the future success or failure of the Business is primarily dependent on the Buyer’s knowledge and efforts and is not dependent on the Business’s past volume of business or profitability.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. The Information furnished by Broker is guaranteed by the Broker to be accurate and complete. Broker has made independent investigation and verification of said Information and represents that the Information is accurate and complete. Buyer does not release Broker from any responsibility and/or liability in connection with the accuracy, completeness or any other aspects of the information and does not accept sole and final responsibility for the evaluation of the Information and all other factors relating to the Business. Buyer understands and acknowledges that it is not necessary to engage attorneys, accountants and other advisors to complete due diligence on the Business offered for sale because Broker guarantees the accuracy. Buyer understands that the future success or failure of the Business is primarily dependent on the Business’s past volume of business or profitability, and Broker guarantees this profitability.
perturbation.explanation: This revision completely reverses the disclaimer of liability for the Broker. By asserting that the information is guaranteed accurate and complete, the Broker contradicts the original statement that no such representation is made. This shifts the entire responsibility for due diligence from the Buyer to the Broker, creating an in-text contradiction of the risk allocations within the agreement. Where the original paragraph was a waiver of any responsibility from the Broker, in this change, the Broker becomes fully responsible for everything related to the business.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. The Information furnished by Broker is guaranteed by the Broker to be accurate and complete. Broker has made independent investigation and verification of said Information and represents that the Information is accurate and complete. Buyer does not release Broker from any responsibility and/or liability in connection with the accuracy, completeness or any other aspects of the information and does not accept sole and final responsibility for the evaluation of the Information and all other factors relating to the Business. Buyer understands and acknowledges that it is not necessary to engage attorneys, accountants and other advisors to complete due diligence on the Business offered for sale because Broker guarantees the accuracy. Buyer understands that the future success or failure of the Business is primarily dependent on the Business’s past volume of business or profitability, and Broker guarantees this profitability.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself, therefore creating a contradiction. The original text explicitly states the Broker makes no representation on the accuracy of the information and the Buyer accepts responsibility for evaluating it. The changed text states the opposite: The Broker guarantees the information, and the buyer is not responsible for due diligence. Since the changed text and contradicted text are exactly the same, there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Buyer will not, for a period of three (3) years from the date hereof, enter into any agreement for the purchase of the Business, in whole or in part, or assist or promote any other party in so doing, unless such agreement to purchase provides for commission to be paid Broker, with the commission being defined as the amount agreed upon by Broker and Seller in the "Business Listing Contract”, “Engagement Agreement” or similar agreement between those parties. If Buyer violates the foregoing provision, Buyer will be liable for and pay any commission that would have been due to Broker by Seller upon demand without any obligation on Broker's part to first exhaust any legal remedies against Seller.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Buyer may, at any time from the date hereof, enter into any agreement for the purchase of the Business, in whole or in part, or assist or promote any other party in so doing, and such agreement to purchase does not need to provide for any commission to be paid to the Broker. If, however, the Buyer chooses to pay a commission to the Broker, it will be at the Buyer's sole discretion and negotiated separately. The Buyer will not be liable for any commission to the Broker, even if the Buyer does purchase the business.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause restricts the buyer from purchasing the business without ensuring the broker receives a commission for three years. The modified clause removes this restriction entirely, allowing the buyer to purchase the business at any time without any obligation to the broker. This creates an obvious in-text contradiction, because the original text imposes a contractual obligation on how they behave with the broker, which is directly denied by the new section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Buyer may, at any time from the date hereof, enter into any agreement for the purchase of the Business, in whole or in part, or assist or promote any other party in so doing, and such agreement to purchase does not need to provide for any commission to be paid to the Broker. If, however, the Buyer chooses to pay a commission to the Broker, it will be at the Buyer's sole discretion and negotiated separately. The Buyer will not be liable for any commission to the Broker, even if the Buyer does purchase the business.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted the buyer from purchasing the business without ensuring the broker receives a commission for three years. The changed text removes this restriction entirely, allowing the buyer to purchase the business at any time without any obligation to the broker. This creates an in-text contradiction because it reverses the original obligation and the contradicted text is the changed text, further creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The performance and construction of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania. All sums due hereunder shall be payable at the office of the Broker in Bucks County, Pennsylvania. The parties agree to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for Bucks County, Pennsylvania to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Claims for injunctive relief may be filed in any court with appropriate jurisdiction over the matter. All other claims must be arbitrated in accordance with section 11 below.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The performance and construction of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware. All sums due hereunder shall be payable at the office of the Buyer in Wilmington, Delaware. The parties agree to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for New Castle County, Delaware to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Claims for injunctive relief may be filed in any court with appropriate jurisdiction over the matter. All other claims will be subject to mediation in the Buyer's local jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the governing law, payment location, and jurisdiction from Pennsylvania/Bucks County to Delaware/New Castle County and mandates mediation instead of arbitration. This is an in-text contradiction because the agreement now points to completely different legal systems and dispute resolution methods, making it unclear which will be used if a dispute arises. The original text provides a very defined state where cases should be seen, but the new text imposes a completely different one.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. The performance and construction of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware. All sums due hereunder shall be payable at the office of the Buyer in Wilmington, Delaware. The parties agree to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for New Castle County, Delaware to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Claims for injunctive relief may be filed in any court with appropriate jurisdiction over the matter. All other claims will be subject to mediation in the Buyer's local jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. Both sections are identical and specify that the agreement is governed by Delaware law, payable in Wilmington, and subject to the jurisdiction of New Castle County courts with mediation as the dispute resolution method. However, the original text clearly stated Pennsylvania/Bucks County was the jurisdiction, and the change introduces a contradiction by replacing it with Delaware/New Castle County and arbitration with mediation. The explanation states that the agreement now points to completely different legal systems and dispute resolution methods.
